findings.summary: There is no control anywhere in the production app — not on the Interview Center screen, not on the Applications screen — to schedule/create an interview. The full backend CRUD (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /interviews, /interviews/{id}/complete, /interviews/{id}/cancel) is implemented, correct, and reachable only via direct API calls; a paying user has no way to reach any of it through the product UI. The Applications screen's interview-stage card link ('View in CRM') routes to /dashboard/networking, not to Interview Center, and no 'Schedule interview' affordance exists anywhere.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123.
2. Visit /dashboard/interviews — only element is 'View Applications' link; no create/schedule button.

3. Visit /dashboard/applications — inspect an interview-stage application card; its only cross-link ('View in CRM') goes to /dashboard/networking (apps/web/src/app/dashboard/applications/page.tsx, CardLink() function), not to Interview Center or any schedule-interview form.
4. Grep the whole apps/web/src tree for any caller of the interviews API — none found besides the unused fetchInterviewPrep() helper in lib/api/workspaces.ts.
findings.expected: A paying user should be able to schedule an interview (from an application card or from Interview Center directly) and see it reflected in the Interview Center prep view, per the wireframe's premise of an active, in-progress interview.
findings.observed: No UI entry point exists to create InterviewSchedule rows. The feature is fully backend-complete but 100% unreachable from the product, i.e. dead on arrival for end users despite working infrastructure.

findings.summary: POST /interviews does not validate that application_id references an existing Application row owned by the caller. Supplying a nonexistent application_id succeeds with HTTP 201 and creates a permanently orphaned InterviewSchedule row (InterviewSchedule.applicationId has no FK constraint — apps/api/app/routers/interviews.py CREATE TABLE, column is plain `text NOT NULL`).
reproduction: 1. Authenticate as admin/admin123, obtain bearer token.
2. POST https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/api/interviews with body {"application_id":"MV-interview-center-verify2-bogus-app","scheduled_at":"2026-08-11T03:00:00Z"} (no such application exists).
3. Observe HTTP 201 Created with a full interview object referencing the bogus application_id.
4. GET /interviews confirms the orphaned row is listed like any real one.
Reproduced twice in independent sessions (interview ids c4fcb3bb3f5c4c245c37117a3 and ca138a62b952fae2b79811283), both times 201; both cleaned up via DELETE afterward.
findings.expected: Creating an interview against a non-existent application should fail with 404/422, matching the pattern used elsewhere in the codebase (e.g. resume/job FK checks) — the code comment in InterviewCreate even states application_id 'is NOT NULL' is enforced 'so a missing value must 422... never reach the INSERT and crash', implying referential validity was intended but only presence, not existence, is checked.
findings.observed: Any string is accepted as application_id; the row inserts successfully, is retrievable/editable/completable/cancelable/deletable like a normal interview, silently orphaned from any real application (this endpoint is not reachable via the current UI, so real-world exposure is limited to internal tooling / a future UI regaining access to POST /interviews).

findings.summary: "Add Contact" (the screen's primary CTA) is 100% client-side fake — it never calls POST /networking/contacts. The new contact appears to save (modal closes, card shows in the "New" column) but is pure ephemeral React state; a reload permanently loses it.
reproduction: Login as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/networking.
Click "+ Add Contact".
Fill Name="MV-networking-Contact-<ts>", Role, Company; click "Save Contact".
Observe: modal closes, contact card appears immediately in the pipeline's New column.
Open browser devtools network tab / capture: zero POST requests to /networking/contacts (or any endpoint) fired during the click.
Reload the page (F5).
Observe: the contact is gone. GET /networking/contacts confirms zero rows were ever created.
findings.expected: Save Contact persists the contact via POST /networking/contacts (the documented, working backend endpoint); the contact survives reload and is visible via GET /networking/contacts.
findings.observed: No network call fires on Save. Contact exists only in local component state (`added` array in page.tsx). Confirmed gone after reload in two independent fresh sessions.

findings.summary: Outreach Queue and Communication Log cards do not render real backend data correctly: a field-name contract mismatch between the frontend TS interface / JSX (`to`/`subject`/`preview`/`tone` and `when`/`who`/`channel`/`note`) and the actual GET /workspaces/networking/summary response (`contactName`/`company`/`subject`/`kind`/`status`/`scheduledAt`/`sentAt`) leaves most fields blank. The Communication Log is effectively illegible even with real "sent" data present.
reproduction: Login, create a real contact via POST /networking/contacts (e.g. name=MV-networking-Test, company=MV Co).
Create a real outreach task via POST /networking/outreach with contact_id=<that contact>, type=follow_up (stays status=pending).
Navigate to /dashboard/networking.

Observe the Outreach Queue card: subject line ("Follow Up — MV Co") renders, but the recipient-name line above it is blank and the `tone:` badge shows "tone:" with no value; message preview line is blank.
PATCH the outreach task to status=sent; reload.
Observe the Communication Log card: entry renders as blank/garbled — no who, no channel, no note text, only separator punctuation (" · ", " — ") visible.
findings.expected: Real outreach/communication data (contact name, message preview, channel/tone, timestamp) renders legibly, since the backend has and returns this data.
findings.observed: Backend response field names never match what the frontend reads; 3 of 4 fields blank in Outreach Queue, 4 of 4 fields blank in Communication Log.

findings.summary: "Import from LinkedIn" button (empty state) does not perform any LinkedIn import or OAuth — it opens the identical manual Add Contact modal as the header's "+ Add Contact" button. No LinkedIn integration is reachable anywhere on this screen. Also a wireframe deviation: wireframe shows two distinct empty-state buttons ("Import from LinkedIn" + "Add Contact Manually"); shipped build has only one, mislabeled button.
reproduction: Login, navigate to /dashboard/networking?demo=empty (or use an account with 0 contacts).
Click "Import from LinkedIn" (LinkedIn-branded button).
Observe: the generic "Add Contact" modal (Name/Role/Company fields) opens — same as manual add, no LinkedIn OAuth redirect, no connection list.
findings.expected: Either a real LinkedIn OAuth/import flow, or (if not built) a button honestly labeled for manual add, or a disabled/coming-soon state — not a mislabeled affordance.
findings.observed: Button click opens the manual single-contact form; label and icon falsely imply bulk LinkedIn import.

findings.summary: "Review all drafts" button in the Outreach Queue card has no click handler at all (confirmed in source, apps/web/src/app/dashboard/networking/page.tsx) — clicking it is a complete dead-end: no navigation, no modal, no console/network event, no visible feedback.
reproduction: Login, seed a real outreach task via POST /networking/outreach so the Outreach Queue renders.
Navigate to /dashboard/networking.
Click "Review all drafts".
Observe: nothing happens — URL unchanged, no modal, no request fired.
findings.expected: Navigates to a drafts review view/modal, or is visually disabled if the feature doesn't exist yet.
findings.observed: Button is inert; live-verified with real outreach data present in two independent sessions.
evidence: test-artifacts/session-a-log.json (STEP4_REVIEW_DRAFTS_BUTTON — button absent in empty state, as expected)

findings.summary: No contact-detail view exists anywhere in the reachable UI. Contact cards are not clickable/interactive. There is no UI control to edit an existing contact's stage, add notes, or set a follow-up reminder — the only ways to mutate a contact (PATCH) or reach a real create (POST, since the modal is fake per MV-networking-001) are direct API calls, unreachable from any button, link, or menu.
reproduction: Login, seed 1-2 real contacts via POST /networking/contacts.
Navigate to /dashboard/networking; observe cards render in the pipeline.
Click a contact card.
Observe: no navigation, no modal, no detail panel — nothing happens.
Search the whole page for any edit/stage-change/notes/reminder control: none exists.
findings.expected: Per the wireframe's product intent ("Recruiter & Referral CRM") and the BRIEF's explicit test scope (contact detail, tags/status/relationship stage, notes, follow-up reminders), a user should be able to open a contact and manage these fields from the UI.
findings.observed: Zero UI surface for contact detail/edit/notes/reminders; entirely read-only display + a non-persisting fake create.
evidence: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/networking/page.tsx (full file reviewed — no onClick/detail affordance on contact-card article)

findings.summary: Pipeline cards omit the wireframe's stage-specific status indicators: wireframe shows a green "Call Thu" schedule badge on Scheduled-stage cards and a "Referred" checkmark badge on Placed-stage cards; shipped build renders only name/role-company/warmth-stars for every stage regardless of stage-specific state.
reproduction: Compare design/screens/networking.html contact-nw11 (Scheduled, shows "Call Thu" badge) and contact-nw12 (Placed, shows "Referred" badge) against the live pipeline for contacts in "meeting"/"referral" stage.
findings.expected: Stage-specific badges present per wireframe.
findings.observed: No such badges exist in the shipped component for any stage.

findings.summary: OutreachTask referential integrity is not enforced despite the DDL declaring `"contactId" text REFERENCES "Contact"("id") ON DELETE CASCADE`. (a) POST /networking/outreach with a nonexistent contact_id returns 201 (should reject). (b) Deleting a Contact does not cascade-delete its OutreachTask rows — orphans remain.
reproduction: POST /networking/outreach with contact_id="nonexistent-mv-test-id" -> observe HTTP 201 (not 404/422).
POST /networking/contacts to create contact C; POST /networking/outreach referencing C -> task T created.
DELETE /networking/contacts/{C.id} -> 204.
GET /networking/outreach -> T is still present, contactId still points at the deleted C.id.
findings.expected: FK constraint rejects orphan creation, and/or cascade-deletes dependent tasks on contact deletion, matching the declared schema.
findings.observed: Neither protection is active in production; orphan OutreachTask rows accumulate silently, which will worsen the illegible-rendering issue in MV-networking-002 over time.
findings.note: BACKEND follow-up (OutreachTask FK/cascade) — out of frontend scope; queue for a backend cluster-E task.

findings.summary: emailAgent is not wired or reachable anywhere on the Networking screen. No "Connect Gmail" affordance, no outreach-drafting AI trigger, no agent-generated draft content exists in the shipped UI (the wireframe's "Agent drafted a warm follow-up..." copy is mockup-only). Since no agent entry point exists, agent-integration testing (real generation vs. fixture, quota decrement, audit fields, honest progress/error states) could not be performed from this screen — there is no human gate to describe because the feature isn't reachable at all, gated or otherwise.
reproduction: Login, navigate to /dashboard/networking (both empty and populated states, incl. with real outreach data).
Inspect full page HTML/DOM and all network calls for any emailAgent trigger, Gmail-connect CTA, or draft-generation action.
None found.
findings.expected: BRIEF flagged emailAgent as possibly wired here; if so, an honest connect/run/paywall-gated flow should be reachable.
findings.observed: No such flow exists on this screen in the current build.
evidence: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/networking/page.tsx (full file reviewed — no agent-run or Gmail-connect code path)
findings.status: ACCEPTED-DEVIATION (LOW visual/wireframe deviation or honestly-labeled substitution; documented in final report, not a fabrication/defect).
findings.note: SCOPE: emailAgent outreach integration — larger effort, deferred.

findings.summary: Clicking "Cancel" in the Add Contact modal closes the modal without adding a contact (correct), but does NOT reset the `form` component state. Reopening the modal shows the previously-typed, cancelled text still populating the Name field.
reproduction: Click "+ Add Contact"; type Name = "MV-networking-ShouldBeDiscarded".
Click "Cancel". Modal closes; contact correctly not added to pipeline.
Click "+ Add Contact" again.
Observe: Name field still shows "MV-networking-ShouldBeDiscarded" from the previous, cancelled attempt.
findings.expected: Reopening after Cancel shows a fresh, empty form.
findings.observed: Stale form state persists across Cancel/reopen. Reproduced identically in two independent fresh runs.

findings.summary: Pressing Escape while the Add Contact modal is open does not close it, despite an `onKeyDown` handler in source specifically checking for `e.key === "Escape"`. (X button and Cancel button both work correctly as alternate close paths.)
reproduction: Click "+ Add Contact" to open the modal.
Press the Escape key.
Observe: modal remains open.
findings.expected: Escape closes the modal (standard dialog behavior; also explicitly coded for in source).
findings.observed: Modal does not close on Escape in production. Reproduced identically in two independent fresh runs. Likely cause: the dialog root never receives programmatic focus on open, so the keydown handler attached to that div never fires (Escape targets document body instead).
evidence: test-artifacts/scripts/nw-session-e-modal-controls.cjs (run twice, identical result both times: escapeCloses:false)

findings.summary: Wireframe's sequential single-item review pattern (back-chevron header, "1 of 2 pending" position counter, implied step-through-the-queue flow) is not implemented on production; production instead always returns to the full scrollable card list after every decision, with no position counter and no auto-advance to the next pending item.
reproduction: Login as admin/admin123 at https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/login (mobile viewport 390x844).
Navigate to /dashboard/approvals with 2+ pending approvals in the queue.
Tap 'Review' on any pending card -> modal opens with header 'Approval Needed' / '{agent} wants to {action}' and an X close icon (no back-chevron, no 'N of M pending' counter anywhere in the modal).

Tap 'Approve & Submit' (or Reject) -> modal closes and returns to the full card list; the next pending item is NOT automatically opened for review, and no position indicator ever appeared during the flow.

Compare against design/screens/mobile-approval.html: header shows a back-chevron (data-design-id=btn-back-ma02) and 'Approval / 1 of 2 pending' (data-design-id=m-topbar-ma01), implying a dedicated single-focus, step-through-the-queue screen.
findings.expected: Per the wireframe, reviewing an approval should feel like a dedicated, focused single-item screen with a back affordance and a position counter ('1 of 2 pending') that helps a user work through multiple pending approvals in sequence.
findings.observed: Production's mobile /dashboard/approvals renders the shared responsive queue-list + bottom-sheet-modal component (ApprovalModal.tsx). The modal has an X-close (not a back-chevron) and no position/count indicator; after a decision the modal simply closes back to the list and the user must manually tap 'Review' on the next card. The list page's own '5 pending' text is an aggregate count only, not a per-item position indicator, and is not visible while the modal is open.
findings.status: ACCEPTED-DEVIATION (LOW visual/wireframe deviation or honestly-labeled substitution; documented in final report, not a fabrication/defect).

findings.summary: The "invalid/stale approval" error message for a broken ?review=<id> deep link is race-conditioned: it is silently swallowed in the majority of real page loads because the pending-list fetch's success handler unconditionally calls setError(null), which can run after (and clear) the error set by the failed single-approval fetch.
reproduction: Fresh browser session, mobile viewport 390x844, login as admin/admin123.
Navigate directly to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/approvals?review=<any-nonexistent-id> (a stale/garbage id).
GET /api/approvals/<id> correctly 404s (confirmed via network capture: {"detail":"Approval not found"}).
Poll the DOM every 100ms for up to 6-8s for a [role=alert] element containing 'The linked approval request could not be found.'

Result across 7 independent fresh-session repro attempts: the error banner was observed in only 2/7 runs; in the other 5/7 it never appeared at all -- the user lands on the plain, unannotated approvals queue with zero indication the deep link was broken.

Root cause confirmed in apps/web/src/app/dashboard/approvals/page.tsx: the review-param effect's .catch() calls setError('...could not be found.'), but the separate load() effect's success path unconditionally calls setError(null) after GET /approvals?status=pending resolves -- whichever promise settles last wins, and the list fetch usually wins the race.
findings.expected: A user following any stale/expired/mistyped approval deep link (e.g. from an old notification, bookmark, or shared link) should reliably see the existing, already-implemented error copy explaining the link is invalid.
findings.observed: The error message is nondeterministic: present in 2/7 fresh-session repro attempts, silently absent in 5/7. This is an unsurfaced failure -- the request genuinely fails (404) but the failure is usually never communicated to the user.

findings.summary: AI Intelligence / recruiter scoring never appears for any email in production — hardcoded null/zero at the API layer — even though a real Gmail account is connected and the underlying emailAgent 'insights' mode works correctly when invoked directly.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 at https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/login and navigate to /dashboard/email.
2. Confirm the Inboxes bar shows 'melbvicduque@gmail.com — Primary' (Gmail IS connected).
3. Click the 'All Recruiter' tab to see the 64 real synced messages.
4. Click any of the first 5 email cards.
5. Observe the 'AI Intelligence' panel and the score badge on the card/detail header.
6. Repeat for a fresh browser session (session2) — identical result.
7. (Backend corroboration) GET /workspaces/emails/inbox returns "score":0 and "intelligence":null for literally every message, unconditionally (apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py L389/L393).

8. (Agent corroboration) POST /agents/email/run {"mode":"insights","thread_id":"c075a8f1ba482d194fc8a0d83"} returns a REAL, non-fixture LLM score/breakdown/summary via the emailAgent, proving the capability works but is never called by the Email Center UI or its backing endpoint.
findings.expected: Per the wireframe (email-center.html #ai-intel-em33) and the emailAgent's documented 'insights' mode, each recruiter email should show a real Interview Conversion Score, a 4-metric breakdown, and a narrative summary — and the copy should never claim Gmail isn't connected when it is.
findings.observed: Every one of 64+ real synced threads shows a 0-score badge and 'No intelligence available yet — connect your Gmail account to enable AI scoring on your real threads.' — even though Gmail IS connected. GET /workspaces/emails/inbox hardcodes score:0 and intelligence:null for all messages; the endpoint never calls the emailAgent. Calling POST /agents/email/run mode=insights directly proves the agent itself works and returns real output (score 20, breakdown, summary, run_id c2037df705848d6adfe1e44a3, model deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro, tokensIn 415/tokensOut 203) — this is a pure UI/endpoint wiring gap, not an agent failure.

findings.summary: AI Draft Reply / Send-Reply gate is completely unreachable from the UI for every one of 66+ real and freshly-created threads across two independent fresh sessions — draftReply is hardcoded to an empty string at the API layer, so the 'Send Reply' button and send-confirmation gate never render, and the UI instead shows the misleading fixed copy 'No reply needed for this email.' for every single thread.
reproduction: 1. Log in and go to /dashboard/email, click 'All Recruiter'.
2. Click through every one of the 64-66 visible email cards (scripted, both fresh sessions).
3. For each, check for [data-testid="draft-reply"] / [data-testid="open-send-gate-btn"].
4. Result: 0 of 65 cards (session1) and 0 of 66 cards (session2) ever show the draft-reply panel or Send Reply button; all show 'No reply needed for this email.'

5. (Backend corroboration) GET /workspaces/emails/inbox hardcodes "draftReply": "" for every message (apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py L394); the frontend's own guard is `{selected.draftReply ? :

No reply needed for this email.

}` (apps/web/src/app/dashboard/email/page.tsx L557).

6. (Agent corroboration) POST /agents/email/run {"mode":"draft_reply","thread_id":"c075a8f1ba482d194fc8a0d83"} returns a real, resume-grounded, non-fixture draft ('Thank you for advancing my application for the Insights Analyst role. I will complete the assessments as instructed.', run_id ca64b35113ad514c79dace495) — proving the emailAgent itself works; the Email Center page never calls /agents/email/run at all (grep confirms zero references).

7. Because the Send Reply button can never render, the two-step send-confirmation gate specified in the wireframe (email-center.html #send-gate-em48) is likewise never reachable through the UI, for any thread, regardless of Gmail-connection state.
findings.expected: Per the wireframe, a real AI-drafted reply (grounded in resume + thread, Voice-DNA scored) should appear for recruiter emails that warrant a reply, with Send/Edit/Preview/Regenerate actions leading into the two-step send-confirmation gate.
findings.observed: Draft reply never appears for ANY thread (0/65 and 0/66 across two fresh sessions); the UI's honest-sounding 'No reply needed for this email.' copy is shown universally, implying an AI judgement that was never actually made — the field is simply always empty by construction, not by AI decision. Consequently the Send-Reply confirmation gate (the screen's headline safety feature per its own subtitle 'Every outbound reply passes the send confirmation gate') is structurally unreachable from the UI today.

findings.summary: The default landing tab ('Priority') is permanently empty for every user because thread classification is never populated in production — a paying user opens Email Center to 'No emails in this view' despite 64+ real synced messages, and must know to manually click 'All Recruiter' to see anything.
reproduction: 1. Log in, navigate to /dashboard/email.
2. Observe the Smart Inbox panel loads with the 'Priority' tab pre-selected (component default state).
3. Observe '[data-testid="inbox-empty"] No emails in this view.' is shown.
4. Click 'Follow-Up Due', 'Auto-Replied', 'Trashed' — each is also empty.
5. Click 'All Recruiter' — 64+ real messages appear.

6. (Backend corroboration) every message's `classification` column is null; GET /workspaces/emails/inbox defaults `"category": t.get("classification") or "all"` so nothing can ever match "priority"/"followup"/"auto"/"trashed" — those 4 of 5 tabs are permanently dead ends because no triage ever runs against real data.
7. Reproduced identically in session1 and session2 (fresh browser contexts).
findings.expected: Real recruiter emails should be triaged into Priority/Follow-Up/Auto-Replied categories (per the emailAgent's documented 'triage' mode and the wireframe's default Priority-tab view), so a paying user's first view of their inbox shows their most important emails, not an empty state.
findings.observed: Priority/Follow-Up Due/Auto-Replied/Trashed tabs show '0 cards / No emails in this view' 100% of the time; only 'All Recruiter' (category='all' catch-all) ever shows content. A workaround exists (click All Recruiter) so this is HIGH rather than BLOCKER, but the default first-impression experience is a broken-looking empty inbox.

findings.summary: The Smart Inbox column has no internal scroll container, pagination, or virtualization — with 64+ real threads it renders to ~8840px tall (whole page ~9138px), breaking the wireframe's fixed-height 3-column layout with an internally-scrolling email list.
reproduction: 1. Log in, go to /dashboard/email, click 'All Recruiter'.
2. Take a full-page screenshot — the Smart Inbox column stretches far below the other two columns.

3. Programmatic check: `getComputedStyle(document.querySelector('[data-testid=smart-inbox]'))` returns `overflowY: 'visible'`, `maxHeight: 'none'`, `height: '8840px'`; `document.body.scrollHeight` = 9138px.

4. Compare to wireframe (email-center.html #inbox-em12): `class="... flex flex-col"` with the email-list div `class="flex flex-col gap-2.5 overflow-y-auto pr-1"` inside a fixed-viewport-height column.
findings.expected: Smart Inbox should be a fixed-height panel (matching the other two columns) with its own internal scrollbar, per the wireframe.
findings.observed: No max-height/overflow constraint exists on the Smart Inbox section; the panel — and therefore the whole page — grows unbounded with the number of threads.

findings.summary: 'This Week's Email Stats' and 'Automated Follow-Ups' panels are permanently hardcoded to zero/empty at the API layer regardless of real inbox activity, and the Follow-Ups empty-state copy also incorrectly tells an already-connected user to 'connect your Gmail account'.
reproduction: 1. Log in, go to /dashboard/email (Gmail already connected, 64+ real threads).
2. Observe 'This Week's Email Stats': Received=64 (real) but Recruiter=0, Auto-drafted=0, Sent (approved)=0, Follow-ups=0, Avg response=0h — all zero regardless of real content.
3. Observe 'Automated Follow-Ups' panel: 'No follow-ups queued yet — connect your Gmail account to enable the follow-up engine on real threads.' — shown even though Gmail IS connected.

4. (Backend corroboration) GET /workspaces/emails/inbox hardcodes `"recruiterEmails": 0, "autoDrafted": 0, "sentApproved": 0, "followUpsSent": 0, "avgResponseHrs": 0` and `"followUps": []` unconditionally (apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py L430-438).
findings.expected: Stats should reflect real inbox activity once emails are synced/triaged; empty-state copy should never claim Gmail is disconnected when an account is actively connected.
findings.observed: Stats and follow-ups are always zero/empty by construction, and the copy is misleading for an already-connected account.

findings.summary: Multiple controls specified in the wireframe are entirely absent from the production Email Center — not degraded, not empty-stated, simply not built: 'Trash Automated/Spam', 'Mark All Read', 'Bulk Label' smart-actions; the Sort control (Interview Score/Career Potential/Recency); the 'Show thread history' expander; the Enthusiasm/Formality/Detail tone-breakdown bars; and the fixed bottom automation-status bar (Last scan/Next scan/Auto-reply mode toggle/Follow-up engine/Spam filter counter).
reproduction: 1. Compare design/screens/email-center.html against apps/web/src/app/dashboard/email/page.tsx and the live DOM at /dashboard/email.

2. grep page.tsx for: 'Trash Automated' (0 matches), 'Mark All Read' (0), 'Bulk Label' (0), 'Sort:' (0), 'thread history' (0), 'Enthusiasm'/'Formality' (0), 'Last scan'/'Next scan'/'Auto-reply mode'/'Spam filter' (0 each).

3. Visually confirm absence in test-artifacts/session1-01-initial-load.png and session1-04-email-selected-detail.png — no smart-action row above the category tabs, no sort row, no thread-history toggle under the email body, no tone sliders under the (non-rendering) draft panel, no fixed footer bar.
findings.expected: Wireframe-specified controls should either be built, or — if intentionally descoped — the wireframe should be marked as such; currently a 3rd-party observer cannot tell these were descoped vs. simply broken.
findings.observed: None of the listed controls exist anywhere in the shipped page.tsx or the live DOM.
findings.status: SCOPE-DEFERRED (net-new smart-action UI: trash/sort/thread-history/automation-bar — not honesty/correctness defects)

findings.summary: The 'LinkedIn Profile' link in the email-detail header is hardcoded to the generic https://www.linkedin.com/ homepage for every message, regardless of which sender/recruiter is selected — not a real per-sender deep link.
reproduction: 1. Select any email in the Smart Inbox.
2. Inspect the 'LinkedIn Profile' link in the detail header.
3. grep apps/web/src/app/dashboard/email/page.tsx for the anchor: `href="https://www.linkedin.com/"` is a literal constant, not derived from `selected`.
findings.expected: The link should deep-link to the actual sender's LinkedIn profile (or be omitted/disabled when no such data exists), per the wireframe's per-recruiter 'Recruiter Profile' card intent.
findings.observed: Same generic https://www.linkedin.com/ URL for every message, which reads as a working, sender-specific affordance but never is.

findings.summary: "Add Offer" is entirely client-side and non-persistent — no backend write of any kind occurs. The API surface for /offers exposes only GET (confirmed in apps/api/app/routers/offers.py and apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py — no POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE route is registered anywhere for offers). The frontend's AddOfferModal.onAdd() only appends to local React state (apps/web/src/app/dashboard/offers/page.tsx: `setAdded((prev) => [...prev, offer])`); it never calls fetch/apiRequest. A user who adds one or more real offers, then reloads the page, navigates to any other route and back, or even just browses away in-app, silently loses every added offer with zero warning, zero error, zero confirmation dialog. The button, the form, the success-looking 'New' badge and computed 'Total comp' all present as if the action succeeded and was saved.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 at https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/login
2. Navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/offers (empty state, 0 offers)
3. Click 'Add Offer', fill Company='MV-offer-Acme', Base=200000, Bonus=20000, Equity=30000, Location='Sydney · Hybrid', click 'Add offer'
4. Observe: modal closes, a new offer card 'MV-offer-Acme' with a 'New' badge, Total comp $250k, Fit score 'Pending' appears in the grid — looks fully successful
5. Open browser devtools Network tab (or capture via Playwright) — confirm NO POST/PUT request was fired to any endpoint during step 3 (only the original GET /api/workspaces/offers from page load)
6. Reload the page (F5) — the MV-offer-Acme card is gone; the screen reverts to the empty state
7. Directly call GET https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/api/workspaces/offers with the admin bearer token — response shows "offers": [] confirming nothing was ever created server-side

8. Alternatively: add an offer, then use in-app navigation (click 'Dashboard' in sidebar, then browser Back) instead of a hard reload — the offer is lost exactly the same way, proving it is pure ephemeral component state, not even a client-side cache
findings.expected: Adding an offer is the core CRUD action of an 'Offer Comparison' screen; a paying user would reasonably expect it to be saved to their account and to still be there next time they open the app (as the wireframe's own populated state — Canva/Atlassian/ANZ — implies: durable, named, real offers). At minimum, a client-only/draft implementation should say so honestly (e.g. 'not saved yet' banner) rather than presenting a full success UI.
findings.observed: The offer is only ever held in transient React component state. No endpoint exists to persist it. Reload, back/forward navigation, or simply leaving the tab all silently destroy the data with no warning to the user.

findings.summary: Negotiation Coach's 'Suggested counter' and the generated 'Draft counter email' are broken/nonsensical: they always show '$0 base' and instruct the user to email a hiring manager asking to discuss 'a base of $0'. Root cause: the backend (both GET /workspaces/offers and GET /offers) hardcodes `negotiation.suggestedCounter: None` unconditionally (apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py:614, apps/api/app/routers/offers.py equivalent) — it is never computed from any real offer data, regardless of how many offers exist. The frontend's NegotiationCoach.tsx coalesces the null to 0 (`negotiation?.suggestedCounter ?? 0`) and renders it directly into both the summary line and a full pre-filled email draft as if it were a real, considered number.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/offers
2. Add at least one offer via 'Add Offer' (e.g. MV-offer-Acme, base 200000) so the Negotiation Coach panel renders (it is only shown when offers.length > 0)
3. Observe the panel: 'Suggested counter: $0 base'
4. Click 'Draft counter email'
5. Observe the generated email text: '...I'd like to discuss a base of $0. I'm confident the AI delivery experience I bring maps directly to the roadmap we discussed...'
6. Confirm via API: curl GET /api/workspaces/offers (or /api/offers) with a valid bearer token — response always contains "negotiation":{"suggestedCounter":null,...} regardless of offers present
7. Repeated in a second, fully fresh browser session — identical '$0 base' result
findings.expected: A 'Negotiation Coach' feature marketed with copy like 'get negotiation coaching tailored to your priorities' (empty-state) should either compute a real, sane counter figure from the user's actual offer data, or — if that capability isn't implemented — omit the counter/email-draft feature entirely rather than emit a specific-sounding but false number that a user could copy-paste into a real negotiation email.
findings.observed: suggestedCounter is unconditionally null server-side; the UI renders it as a literal '$0' in both the summary chip and the auto-generated draft email, in every account state, real or empty.
evidence: screens/offer-comparison/test-artifacts/run2__results.json (weights_and_negotiation_panels — suggestedCounter absent from initial fetch too)

findings.summary: The Add-Offer modal's full-screen backdrop does not actually cover the full viewport: a full-width strip roughly 24 CSS px tall at the very top of the screen is left uncovered, even though the wrapper div is `fixed inset-0 z-50` with computed style top:0/right:0/bottom:0/left:0 (getBoundingClientRect nonetheless reports top:24, height:696 of a 720px-tall viewport — a rendering/hit-test discrepancy, not merely a CSS authoring mistake, since the applied inset is genuinely 0 by computed style). Background elements in that strip (the sidebar's top row and, critically, live Topbar controls including the Notifications link) remain both visible (undimmed in a full-page screenshot) and clickable while the modal is open with aria-modal="true". Clicking the Notifications bell's top edge while the Add-Offer modal is open, mid-draft, navigates the whole page away to /dashboard/approvals — silently destroying the in-progress modal and any unsaved form input, with no confirmation.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/offers
2. Click 'Add Offer' to open the modal; type something into the Company field (e.g. 'MV-offer-unsaved-work-probe')
3. Via devtools console (or Playwright): document.elementFromPoint(5,5) — returns the sidebar <aside> element, not the modal backdrop <div>
4. document.elementFromPoint(1074,13) (over the Notifications bell's top edge) — returns the notifications <a> link itself, aria-label 'Notifications — 5 pending approvals'
5. Click at those coordinates (mouse.click(1074, 13)) — the browser navigates to /dashboard/approvals; the Add-Offer modal and its draft text are gone with no warning

6. Take a full-page screenshot with the modal open — visually confirm the topbar/header band ('Good afternoon, Administrator...') is NOT dimmed by the black/blur overlay that darkens the rest of the page
7. Reproduced identically across two separate fresh browser sessions (run2 automated pass) plus 5 additional independent investigative sessions — 100% consistent
findings.expected: An aria-modal="true" dialog with a full-screen backdrop should make the entire rest of the page visually inert and non-interactive — no gap, no click-through, no accidental navigation destroying in-progress form input. This is the same class of issue previously tracked as GAP-P4-057 (tab-order leak), except this is a pointer/hit-test leak in the same modal.
findings.observed: A reproducible ~24px full-width band at the very top of the viewport is excluded from the modal's effective overlay coverage; real topbar controls there remain clickable and can navigate the user away, discarding the modal and any unsaved draft.
evidence: screens/offer-comparison/test-artifacts/investigate-modal-overlay-gap-visual.png (full-page screenshot showing undimmed top band while modal is open)
screens/offer-comparison/test-artifacts/scripts/backdrop-investigate4.cjs, backdrop-investigate6.cjs, backdrop-investigate9.cjs, backdrop-investigate10.cjs (isolation scripts + console output)
apps/web/src/components/offers/AddOfferModal.tsx:143-151 (fixed inset-0 z-50 wrapper + absolute inset-0 backdrop)

findings.summary: The screen's own subtitle ('Weighted decision analysis across your live offers') and the 'Priority Weights' panel imply an interactive, computed ranking — but the weights (Compensation 30% / Growth 25% / Culture 20% / Location & flexibility 15% / Company stability 10%) are entirely static: there is no input control to adjust them (PriorityWeights.tsx renders read-only progress bars only), and no code anywhere computes a weighted score from them. The only per-offer number shown ('Fit score') is either the pre-existing, unrelated Job.fitScore field from job-matching (for real backend-sourced offers) or simply 'Pending' (for any offer added via the Add-Offer modal) — it is never derived from, or affected by, the Priority Weights panel in any way. Changing what you 'care about' has literally no effect anywhere on the screen because there is no mechanism to change it at all.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/offers
2. Read the page subtitle: 'Weighted decision analysis across your live offers'
3. Inspect the Priority Weights panel — Compensation/Growth/Culture/Location/Stability bars with fixed percentages, no sliders, no inputs, no drag handles, no edit affordance of any kind
4. Inspect apps/web/src/components/offers/PriorityWeights.tsx — confirm no onChange/interactive elements exist
5. Inspect apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py:602-608 — confirm the 5 weight rows are hardcoded literals returned on every request, not read from or written by any user preference
6. Confirm Fit score per offer card is independent of the weights: real offers show Job.fitScore verbatim; client-added offers always show 'Pending' regardless of the (immutable) weights
findings.expected: A 'weighted decision analysis' feature should let the user express what they actually prioritize and see the ranking/fit-score respond to it — or, if that's not implemented yet, should not present a specific numeric breakdown (30/25/20/15/10) as though it is live and meaningful.
findings.observed: Weights are decorative and fixed; no scoring logic anywhere consumes them.

findings.summary: There is no way to remove or edit an offer once added — no delete/remove icon or edit affordance exists anywhere on an offer card, in either the wireframe or the shipped UI (they agree with each other here, so this is not a wireframe deviation, only a product-completeness gap). Combined with MV-offer-comparison-001 (no persistence), a user who mistypes a company name or salary during Add Offer has no in-product way to correct or remove just that one entry — full reload wipes everything, not just the bad entry.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/offers
2. Add an offer with a typo, e.g. Company='MV-offer-Acme' but meant to type a different name
3. Inspect the resulting offer card (apps/web/src/components/offers/OfferCard.tsx) — no delete/edit button, icon, or menu is rendered anywhere on the card

4. Confirm the wireframe (design/screens/offer-comparison.html, offer-card-of07/08/09) also has no such control — so this is a coverage gap against reasonable product expectations, not a build-vs-wireframe defect
findings.expected: A comparison tool would reasonably let a user remove or correct a single entry without losing the rest of their work.
findings.observed: No edit/delete UI exists on offer cards.
findings.owner: fix/mv-j6-backend @84ea1e2 — NO CODE (delete already ownership-scoped+404 anti-enum+wired UI; wireframe has no edit affordance); reviewer a06d47b1 confirmed
findings.note: DELETE endpoint added w/ ownership check (404 anti-enumeration) — partial mitigation, per plan-review.

findings.summary: All dollar figures on the screen are shown with a bare '$' and no currency code (e.g. '$250k'), even though the backend computes a currency-qualified `salaryRange` field (e.g. 'AUD 185,000–220,000') per real offer — the frontend Offer type/OfferCard never reads or renders `salaryRange` at all, so the computed field is dead weight and the UI never discloses which currency the numbers are in. Not a fabricated-math issue (no tax/take-home calculation is present on this screen to verify against AUD/GST reality — confirmed absent from both the wireframe and the implementation), just an omitted disambiguation on an Australia-focused platform where offers could plausibly be in AUD, USD, NZD, etc.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/offers, add any offer
2. Observe the offer card shows only '$NNNk' style figures with no currency code anywhere on the card

3. Inspect apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py:591-593 — `salaryRange` is computed with a currency prefix (defaulting 'AUD') but apps/web/src/lib/api/workspaces.ts's Offer type and OfferCard.tsx never consume/render it
findings.expected: Given the computed backend field exists specifically to disambiguate currency, the UI should surface it, or otherwise label the currency once at the screen level.
findings.observed: salaryRange (with currency code) is computed server-side but never displayed.

findings.summary: All three Notifications-tab toggles ("Approval requests", "Application updates", "Weekly digest") are completely non-functional decorative controls. Clicking any of them never changes their state, yet the page's global "Save Changes" button still returns HTTP 200 and displays a "Settings saved ✓" confirmation, giving the user false assurance that their notification preference was changed.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 on production and navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/settings.
2. Click the 'Notifications' sub-nav tab (data-testid=settings-nav-notifications).
3. Read the DOM state of the 'Weekly digest' switch: document.querySelector('[data-testid="toggle-notif-digest"]').getAttribute('aria-checked') -> "false".
4. Click the switch (via a real Playwright locator.click(), which performs full actionability checks, AND separately via a native el.click() JS call).
5. Re-read aria-checked -> still "false" (unchanged). Same result for toggle-notif-approvals (stays "true") and toggle-notif-apps (stays "true").

6. Click 'Save Changes' -> UI shows 'Settings saved ✓' and PUT /api/workspaces/settings returns 200, but the PUT payload schema (SettingsUpdate: profile + agentConfig only) has no field for notification preferences at all, and GET /api/workspaces/settings never returns a 'notifications' key.

7. Source-level root cause confirmed at apps/web/src/app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx:504-509: all three <Toggle> elements are given hardcoded value={true}/value={false} props and onChange={() => undefined} (a literal no-op).

8. Repeated the entire sequence in a brand-new, isolated browser context (VERIFY TWICE) with a fresh login -> identical result (aria-checked unchanged for both toggle-notif-digest and toggle-notif-approvals).
findings.expected: Clicking a notification toggle should flip its visual state and persist the new preference (via Save Changes), matching the interactive appearance of an on/off switch.
findings.observed: The three Notifications toggles never change state under any click method (Playwright locator click, raw mouse click at exact coordinates, or native DOM .click()), across two independent sessions. No console error is thrown; the failure is completely silent, and the surrounding 'Save Changes' flow reports success regardless.

findings.summary: The 'Job Board Integrations' Sync / Sync All buttons (Greenhouse, Ashby, Lever, Remoteok, Remotive, and the top-level 'Sync All') are entirely decorative client-side theater: clicking them fires zero network requests and never actually re-syncs anything, yet displays a convincing 'Syncing…' spinner and a temporary 'syncing' status badge for 1.5 seconds before silently reverting -- misleading the user into believing a real integration refresh occurred.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /dashboard/settings, click the 'Integrations' sub-nav tab.
2. Record the visible text for the Greenhouse row: 'Greenhouse / 16 jobs discovered · last sync 2026-07-16 18:32 UTC / connected / Sync'.
3. With network request capture active, click the 'Sync' button for Greenhouse (data-testid=sync-greenhouse).
4. Observe the UI immediately shows a 'syncing' badge and 'Syncing…' text with a spinner icon, and the Sync button becomes a disabled '...' icon.
5. After ~1.5s the row silently reverts to its original 'connected' / 'Sync' appearance.
6. Inspect the captured network log for the entire interaction window: zero requests to any /api/* endpoint were made.
7. Re-read the row text: identical to step 2, byte-for-byte ('last sync 2026-07-16 18:32 UTC' unchanged).

8. Source-level root cause confirmed at apps/web/src/app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx:174-177: syncOne(name) only does setSyncing(...true) then setTimeout(()=>setSyncing(...false), 1500) -- no fetch/axios call anywhere in the function.
9. Repeated in a brand-new isolated browser session (VERIFY TWICE) with fresh login -> identical result: zero network calls, row text unchanged.
findings.expected: Clicking 'Sync' for a job board should call a real backend refresh endpoint and, on completion, update the 'last sync' timestamp / job count from live data (as the Career Data 'Sync now' button on the same screen genuinely does, calling POST /api/workspaces/career-data/refresh).
findings.observed: No backend call is ever made; the 1.5s 'Syncing…' animation is purely a local setTimeout with no effect on any server state. The wireframe's own script comment ('P0-S03 — job board sync affordances (wireframe-level feedback)') shows this fake behavior was carried verbatim from the wireframe mockup into the shipped product without ever being wired to a real endpoint.

findings.summary: The Settings screen's own backing-endpoint matrix lists 4 /billing/* endpoints (GET /billing/entitlement, GET /billing/subscription, POST /billing/checkout, POST /billing/portal), but only the first is ever called, and even its response is never rendered: GET /billing/entitlement fires on every Settings page load and returns real, live Pro-plan/active-status data for this account ({"active_paid":true,"plan":{"id":"pro","status":"active"}}), yet no plan badge, quota indicator, or manage-subscription/cancel/billing-portal/invoice control exists anywhere on the Settings page. This directly corroborates MV-pricing-003 (filed by the pricing screen tester) from the Settings screen's own vantage point -- it is a cross-reference, not a duplicate, since it specifically documents that 3 of the 4 billing endpoints assigned to THIS screen's matrix are never invoked by it at all.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 (account holds a TEMP-granted Pro entitlement) and load /dashboard/settings with network capture active.
2. Confirm GET /api/billing/entitlement fires automatically on page load and returns HTTP 200 {"active_paid":true,"plan":{"id":"pro","status":"active"},"requiresSubscription":true}.

3. Separately call GET /api/billing/subscription directly -> HTTP 200 with full real quota data ({"plan":{"id":"pro","name":"Pro"},"status":"active","quota":{"runsUsed":15,"runsAllowed":100,"spendUsedUsd":0.074688,"spendCapUsd":15.0,...}}) -- this endpoint is never called by the Settings page itself.

4. Enumerate every visible section/heading/button across all 7 Settings tabs (Profile, Resume Management, Portfolio Sync, Notifications, Agent Configuration, Integrations, Privacy & Compliance): none mention 'Pro', 'plan', 'subscription', 'billing', 'quota', 'upgrade', 'downgrade', 'cancel', or 'invoice'.

5. Call POST /api/billing/portal directly -> HTTP 409 {"detail":"No billing account yet — subscribe first"} even though this account's entitlement is active/Pro (the TEMP grant bypassed a real Stripe checkout, so there is no Stripe customer record).

6. Call POST /api/billing/checkout directly -> HTTP 400 {"detail":"This plan is not yet available for purchase (no Stripe price configured)"}, confirming CLM-068's still-blocked Stripe integration from this screen's own endpoint set.
findings.expected: A screen whose own backing-endpoint matrix includes GET /billing/subscription, POST /billing/checkout, and POST /billing/portal should surface at least a current-plan indicator and, ideally, a manage/cancel affordance driven by those endpoints.
findings.observed: 3 of the 4 assigned billing endpoints are never called by the Settings screen; the one that is called (GET /billing/entitlement) has its response silently discarded from the UI. A Pro-plan user has no way to see their plan, usage, or a path to manage/cancel it anywhere on Settings.
evidence: curl transcripts captured this session (billing/entitlement, billing/subscription, billing/portal, billing/checkout) -- see report body §Claim Verdicts CLM-068

findings.summary: The 'Portfolio Sync' sub-nav tab renders a panel whose visible section heading reads 'Resume Management' (byte-identical content to the actual 'Resume Management' tab) rather than anything labelled 'Portfolio Sync' -- a user clicking the 'Portfolio Sync' nav item sees a mismatched heading for the panel they landed on.
reproduction: 1. Log in and navigate to /dashboard/settings.
2. Click 'Resume Management' in the sub-nav -> panel heading reads 'Resume Management', followed by a 'Career Data' section.

3. Click 'Portfolio Sync' in the sub-nav (a different, distinctly-labelled nav item) -> panel heading STILL reads 'Resume Management', followed by the same 'Career Data' section -- pixel-identical to step 2's screenshot.
findings.expected: A distinctly-labelled nav item should render distinctly-labelled (or at least topically consistent) content, or the two nav items should be merged if they are meant to show the same panel.
findings.observed: Both 'Resume Management' and 'Portfolio Sync' nav items render the exact same panel, headed 'Resume Management'.
findings.status: ACCEPTED-DEVIATION (LOW visual/wireframe deviation or honestly-labeled substitution; documented in final report, not a fabrication/defect).

findings.summary: Production Settings deviates from the settings.html wireframe in several presentational ways that are not functional defects but are worth recording for visual-conformance: (a) no 'Change avatar' upload control exists (wireframe shows one; production shows a static initials circle only); (b) the 'Job Board Integrations' section shows a completely different set of sources (Greenhouse/Ashby/Lever/Remoteok/Remotive -- generic ATS platforms) than the wireframe's AU-specific job-seeker boards (Seek/LinkedIn AU/Workforce Australia via MyGov/Jora/Indeed AU), and drops the wireframe's 4-state status legend (Connected/Syncing/Disconnected/Not configured) in favour of a single 'connected' badge for every row; (c) 'Connected Accounts & API Keys' shows OpenRouter / Abacus Subscription (fallback) / Google (Gmail) rather than the wireframe's LinkedIn / GitHub / OpenRouter API set.
reproduction: 1. Compare design/screens/settings.html against a full-page screenshot of https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/settings (test-artifacts/01-settings-load-fullpage.png) side by side.
2. Confirm no avatar-upload button/element exists anywhere in the live DOM (grep of element inventory).
3. Confirm the Job Board Integrations row set and Connected Accounts row set differ from the wireframe's named entries.
findings.expected: Wireframe and production need not be pixel-identical, but component presence and general content categories are expected to correspond per protocol §3.2 point 1.
findings.observed: Structural/nav-label conformance is otherwise strong (all 7 sub-nav labels match exactly), but the specific integrations/accounts shown and the avatar-upload affordance differ from the wireframe.
findings.status: ACCEPTED-DEVIATION (LOW visual/wireframe deviation or honestly-labeled substitution; documented in final report, not a fabrication/defect).

findings.summary: Real cover-letter-agent-generated approvals render an almost-empty review modal: the ApprovalRequest payload created by CoverLetterAgent.run() (apps/api/app/agents/cover_letter_agent.py:659-670) only sets kind/cover_letter_id/job_id/job_title/company, so the modal's 'Why approval is needed', 'AI reasoning', confidence badge, and — critically — the 'Generated cover letter' preview (the actual letter text) never render, because parseApprovalPayload() defaults all of them to null/[] when absent. A paying user reviewing a genuine AI-generated approval cannot see what they are approving in the one screen whose purpose is that review.
reproduction: Log in as admin/admin123 at https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/login
POST /agents/scout/run then GET /jobs to find a job id not already claimed by a pending approval
POST /agents/cover-letter/run {job_id} (async: poll GET /agents/jobs/{job_id} until status=completed) — note the returned approval_id
Navigate to /dashboard/approvals, filter Pending, click Review on the resulting card

Observe: no 'Why approval is needed' box, no 'AI reasoning' checklist, no confidence %, no 'Generated cover letter' preview text — only header, job title/company tile, trust checkbox, and action buttons
findings.expected: Per design/screens/approval-modal.html, the modal shows why the approval fired, AI reasoning bullets, a confidence score, and a preview of the generated letter so the human can make an informed decision.
findings.observed: For approval id c3df4494103f796021d8aae69 (real agent-generated, job 'Innovation Product Manager, Australia' @ harvey), GET /approvals/c3df4494103f796021d8aae69 payload = {kind, cover_letter_id, job_id, job_title, company} only. Live UI: whyVisible=false, reasoningVisible=false, confidenceVisible=false, previewVisible=false (confirmed twice, sessions 1 and 2).

findings.summary: 'Edit & Approve' is permanently disabled for every real cover-letter-agent-generated approval, because ApprovalModal.tsx:326 disables it whenever details.preview === null, and (per finding 001) the real agent payload never sets a preview field. The wireframed edit-before-approve flow is unreachable for genuine data; it only works on hand-crafted payloads that happen to include a preview string.
reproduction: Open the review modal for a real agent-generated approval (see MV-approval-modal-001 steps)
Observe the 'Edit & Approve' button is rendered with disabled styling and is not clickable
Contrast with a payload that DOES include a 'preview' field (e.g. POST /approvals with payload.preview set): 'Edit & Approve' is enabled and the textarea opens correctly
findings.expected: The wireframe's Edit & Approve action lets the reviewer tweak the generated letter before approving.
findings.observed: editDisabled=true for the real approval's modal-edit-btn; editDisabled=false (and fully functional, verified with textarea fill/discard) for a synthetic payload with preview set.

findings.summary: A long/unbroken string in an approval payload's job_title (or company) field breaks the page layout: no CSS word-break/truncate/overflow-wrap guard on the card title, so the whole /dashboard/approvals page (and the Dashboard's 'Needs Approval' widget, which reuses the same summarize() text) overflows horizontally, producing a page-wide horizontal scrollbar and a broken, unreadable layout for every card on the page, not just the offending one.
reproduction: POST /approvals with payload.job_title containing a long run of characters with no whitespace (e.g. 300x 'A')
Navigate to /dashboard/approvals (Pending filter) and to /dashboard
Observe the page body scrolls horizontally far past the viewport and every card layout is pushed/broken
findings.expected: Card text should wrap or truncate; the page should never require horizontal scrolling regardless of payload content.
findings.observed: document.body.scrollWidth = 3901px against a 1440px viewport (verified twice, sessions 1 and 2); visually confirmed in full-page screenshots on both /dashboard/approvals and /dashboard.

findings.summary: parseConfidence() (apps/web/src/components/approvals/lib.ts) does not clamp out-of-range confidence values: a payload confidence of 1.5 (invalid — outside the documented [0,1] fraction range) is rendered as a nonsensical '2%' badge instead of being rejected/hidden.
reproduction: POST /approvals with payload.confidence = 1.5
Open the card/modal and read the confidence badge
findings.expected: An out-of-range confidence value should be treated as absent/invalid (null), not silently rounded into a misleading percentage.
findings.observed: Card and modal both display '2%' for confidence:1.5 (verified twice).

findings.summary: Browser Back, while the review modal is open (opened via the deep-link ?review= param), does not close the modal — it navigates the user away from /dashboard/approvals entirely (observed landing on /dashboard) because syncReviewParam() uses history.replaceState, which never pushes a history entry for modal open/close. A user who reflexively hits Back to dismiss the dialog instead loses their place on the Approvals screen.
reproduction: Navigate to /dashboard/approvals, click Review on any card (URL becomes .../approvals?review=<id>)
Press the browser Back button
Observe the URL and page: it navigates to /dashboard (or whatever preceded Approvals in history), not back to a modal-closed /dashboard/approvals
findings.expected: Back should close the review dialog and return to the Approvals list (or at minimum stay on /dashboard/approvals), consistent with standard modal/dialog navigation semantics.
findings.observed: urlAfterOpen=.../approvals?review=<id> -> urlAfterBack=https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard; modalVisibleAfterBack=false. Reproduced identically in a fresh session.

findings.summary: Deep-linking to a nonexistent approval id (/dashboard/approvals?review=<bad-id>) causes a real 404 from GET /approvals/{id} (confirmed in the server access log and network capture), and the frontend's catch handler does set an error message ('The linked approval request could not be found.') — but it is never shown, because the concurrent approvals-list load() effect's unconditional setError(null) on its own success wins the race and clears it. The failure is silently swallowed: the URL's review param is cleaned up and the page just shows the normal pending list with zero user-visible explanation.
reproduction: While logged in, navigate directly to /dashboard/approvals?review=nonexistent-id-mv
Wait >1s for both the list load and the deep-link fetch to settle
Observe: no modal opens, and no red/alert banner explaining the linked approval could not be found — the page silently reverts to the normal Pending list
findings.expected: Per apps/web/src/app/dashboard/approvals/page.tsx:60-66, a failed deep-link fetch should surface 'The linked approval request could not be found.' in the page's error banner (role=alert).
findings.observed: Network confirms GET /approvals/nonexistent-id-mv[-verify2] -> 404 {"detail":"Approval not found"} in both sessions, but getByRole('alert') / getByText(/could not be found/i) never becomes visible in either session (waited 700ms then 1200ms respectively).
evidence: /var/log/aether/api.log (line containing 'GET /approvals/nonexistent-id-mv-verify2 HTTP/1.1" 404 Not Found', read-only production log excerpt, not copied off-host)

findings.summary: The modal's action-summary/preview section keeps cover-letter-specific copy ('Generated cover letter' label, 'CV'-style avatar fallback) even when the approval type is email_send (an outbound email, not a cover letter/application).
reproduction: POST /approvals with type=email_send and a payload of {to, subject, preview}
Open its review modal
findings.expected: Section copy/iconography should reflect the actual approval type (e.g. 'Outbound email' / recipient+subject), matching the header subtitle which does correctly say 'Email Agent wants to send an email'.
findings.observed: Header subtitle correctly reads 'Email Agent wants to send an email', but the tile still shows generic 'CV' initials + summary text 'Outbound email' (from summarizeType fallback) and the letter-preview box is still headed 'GENERATED COVER LETTER'.
findings.status: ACCEPTED-DEVIATION (LOW visual/wireframe deviation or honestly-labeled substitution; documented in final report, not a fabrication/defect).

findings.summary: POST /approvals/{id}/execute (one of the 5 endpoints in this screen's matrix) is fully implemented and behaves correctly server-side (200 executed when approved; 403 when not approved; 409 when expired — all verified live), but has zero UI wiring: no button, link, or code path anywhere in the approvals list/modal ever calls it. The wireframe likewise has no separate 'execute' affordance.
reproduction: grep apps/web/src for '/execute' or an execute call in the approvals components — none found
Directly call POST /approvals/{approved-id}/execute, /{rejected-id}/execute, /{pending-id}/execute via the API and confirm the documented status codes
findings.expected: N/A — flagged as a coverage note, not necessarily a defect (the endpoint's own docstring says downstream submission integrations land in a later phase).

findings.summary: A genuine production cover-letter generation (job 'Innovation Product Manager, Australia' @ harvey, approval c3df4494103f796021d8aae69) produced a grammatically broken opening sentence — 'My background as an I am a direct match for the Innovation Product Manager, Australia role at harvey.' This is caused by enforce_first_person() in cover_letter_agent.py over-matching: the seeded admin account has name=='Administrator' AND targetRole=='Administrator', so the deterministic hook clause 'as an Administrator' gets rewritten by the third-person-pronoun regex (which targets any literal occurrence of the signer's name) into 'as an I am', mangling a sentence where 'Administrator' was being used as a job-title noun, not a third-person reference to the candidate. Because of MV-approval-modal-001 (no letter preview shown in the real approval's review modal), this broken sentence was invisible to the human reviewer at approval time — it shipped into an approved, submitted Application untouched.
reproduction: POST /agents/cover-letter/run for a job where the resulting letter's deterministic hook includes the candidate's current position/title
Poll GET /agents/jobs/{id} until completed; read result.cover_letter
With the seeded admin/admin123 account (name='Administrator', targetRole='Administrator'), observe the hook sentence is corrupted
findings.expected: The composed business letter should always be grammatically well-formed; enforce_first_person should only rewrite genuine third-person references to the candidate, not a coincidental noun match against the signer's own name string.
findings.observed: cover_letter text begins: 'My background as an I am a direct match for the Innovation Product Manager, Australia role at harvey.' (should read '...as an Administrator is a direct match...'). Confirmed via the raw agent-run result and again via GET /applications/{cover_letter_id} after approval.

2. Record the rendered text of the Dashboard-summary cards, ATS Score Distribution panel, and Agent ROI panel while period='all' is selected

Observe the network requests fired: GET /analytics/funnel?period=7d and GET /analytics/conversion?period=7d fire with the new period; GET /analytics/dashboard, GET /analytics/ats-distribution, and GET /analytics/agent-roi either do not fire a period-scoped variant or (for /analytics/dashboard) fire with NO period query param at all

findings.summary: Dashboard-summary 'Applications' metric card (14) and the Application-Funnel 'Applied' stage (7) show materially different numbers for what a user would reasonably read as the same metric, on the same screen, same account, same period, with no UI cue explaining the discrepancy.
reproduction: 1. Login as admin/admin123 (Pro), navigate to /dashboard/analytics
2. Read the top-left Dashboard-summary card: label 'Applications', value 14, tooltip 'Total roles you've applied to, across all sources and time periods.'
3. Scroll to the 'Application Funnel (all)' section directly below and read the 'Applied' bar: value 7

4. Both are on-screen simultaneously; no annotation clarifies that 'Applications'=14 counts ALL Application rows (including drafts never submitted) while funnel 'Applied'=7 counts only non-draft (submitted) applications
findings.expected: Either both figures use the same definition of 'applications', or the UI clearly differentiates them (e.g. 'Applications (incl. drafts)' vs 'Submitted'), so a paying user auditing their funnel does not see two different totals for what reads as one concept.
findings.observed: Direct SQL recomputation against the production DB for this user confirms the root cause: 14 total Application rows break down as 7 draft + 6 submitted + 1 rejected. Dashboard-summary counts all 14 (apps/api/app/routers/analytics.py _dashboard(): 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM "Application" WHERE "userId"=%s'). Funnel 'applied' counts only the 7 non-draft rows (funnel(): 'COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE "status" <> \'draft\')'). Both numbers are individually real and non-fabricated (0.0% delta vs direct SQL), but the screen presents them side-by-side without disambiguation, which is confusing/misleading for a paying user cross-checking their funnel math.
evidence: test-artifacts/paid/26-tooltip-open-proper.png (shows both '14' Applications card and '7' Applied funnel bar in the same screenshot)
test-artifacts/paid/db-groundtruth-sql-recompute.txt (direct SQL: total_applications=14, funnel applied=7, status breakdown draft=7/submitted=6/rejected=1)

findings.summary: Test Run modal ('Run Test' — POST /agents/test-run) throws a raw, truncated Zod schema-validation error directly into the UI for 18 of the 22 catalog agents selectable in its own dropdown (all 12 'Planned' agents plus 6 real, currently-Active deterministic agents: Job Discovery, ATS Optimization, Match Scoring, Job Matching, Skill Gap, Orchestration). Only the 4 true LLM-backed agents (Resume Tailoring, Cover Letter, Email, Story Extraction) work correctly.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 (canonical login); confirm GET /api/billing/entitlement -> active_paid:true, plan.id:'pro'.
2. Navigate (Playwright, production) to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/agents.
3. Click the header 'Test Run' button (data-testid=test-run-open) to open the Test Run modal.

4. Inspect the 'Select agent' dropdown (data-testid=test-run-select): it lists all 22 catalog agents (options filtered only by `agent.enabled`, which defaults true for every card including Planned ones — see apps/web/src/components/agents/TestRunModal.tsx:29, `agents.filter((a) => a.enabled)`, with no check on `runnable` or `status !== 'planned'`).
5. Select 'Compliance Agent' (a Planned/coming-soon card with no backend) and click 'Run Test' (data-testid=test-run-go).

6. Observe: the network call POST /agents/test-run returns HTTP 200 with body {"model":null,"estTokens":null,"estCost":null,"actualCost":null,"actualTokens":null,"responseSeconds":null,"creditsCharged":0.0} (a legitimate, documented, honest null-shape per the endpoint's own docstring at apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:2178-2224). The frontend's TestRunSchema (apps/web/src/components/agents/api.ts:89-100) declares model/estTokens/estCost/actualCost/actualTokens/responseSeconds as REQUIRED non-nullable z.string()/z.number() with no `.nullable()`; parsing this valid 200 response throws a ZodError, caught by TestRunModal.tsx:96-97 and rendered verbatim (first 140 chars) as the on-screen error: `[{"code":"invalid_type","expected":"string","received":"null","path":["model"],"message":"Expec` (cut off mid-word by the 140-char slice).

7. Repeat step 5-6 with any of: submission, salaryIntelligence, interviewPrep, companyResearch, recruiterOutreach, marketTrends, scheduling, sentimentAnalysis, reference, learningFeedback, notification (the 11 other Planned cards) -> identical raw-JSON error class.

8. Repeat with jobDiscovery, atsOptimization, matchScoring, jobMatching, skillGap, orchestration (6 real, currently 'Active'-status deterministic-backend agents) -> same failure. Root cause confirmed via curl: GET /agents/catalog applies a `model = _model_for_agent(backend) or "deterministic"` fallback (apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:1501) so these show model:'deterministic' on their cards, but POST /agents/test-run (line 2192: `model = _model_for_agent(backend) if backend else None`) omits that same fallback and returns raw null for the identical backend value, so every deterministic (non-LLM) agent breaks Test Run even though its own card and Run button work perfectly.

9. Control: select 'Story Extraction Agent' (a real LLM-backed agent) and click 'Run Test' -> succeeds normally, renders 'Dry-run complete. Agent responded in 22.4s. Actual cost $0.001 · 523 tokens. No credits were charged for this preview.' -- confirming the modal itself, the honest non-billing contract ('creditsCharged:0.0'), and the 4 working agents are genuinely fine; the defect is isolated to the schema/endpoint contract mismatch for the other 18.
10. Reproduced in a SECOND, independent fresh browser session/login with two different agents (atsOptimization and notification) -> identical raw-JSON error both times.
findings.expected: Test Run should either omit Planned/deterministic agents from its own selectable dropdown (mirroring the Run button's own runnable gating on the card), or the frontend schema should honestly accept the documented null shape and show a friendly message (e.g. 'This agent has no LLM cost to preview' for deterministic agents, 'Not yet available' for Planned ones) — never a raw Zod validation object.
findings.observed: 18 of 22 dropdown entries (82%) produce a truncated, technical, machine-readable JSON error string rendered directly in the paying user's UI inside a role="alert" box, violating the 'no raw stack traces/5xx JSON in UI' requirement even though the underlying HTTP response is a clean 200 — the leak is purely a frontend contract bug, not a server error.

findings.summary: The Anthropic Claude provider card permanently shows the green 'Connected · Manage' status/action (status:"connected") even after a live, real 'Test connection' round-trip to api.anthropic.com proves the stored credential is broken (HTTP 401, 'OAuth access token has expired'). The only visible sign of the real failure is a small, neutrally-styled detail line ('...last verify: failed') beneath the still-green badge — a paying user relying on the badge/button color would reasonably believe Anthropic is actively working.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 (Pro entitlement); navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/agents.

2. Observe the Anthropic Claude provider card: green dot, badge 'Saved in app', action button 'Connected · Manage' (green), detail text 'Credential stored in the encrypted vault (…eAAA) · last verify: ok' (a stale prior success).

3. Click 'Connected · Manage' to open the config modal, then click 'Test connection' (data-testid=provider-config-verify) -- a genuine, non-simulated round-trip (POST /agents/providers/anthropic/verify) to api.anthropic.com.

4. Observe the honest failure notice: 'Anthropic Claude connection failed — anthropic returned HTTP 401: {"type":"error","error":{"type":"authentication_error","message":"OAuth access token has expired. Re-authenticate to continue."},"request_id":null}'.

5. Close the modal and re-read the main provider grid card: the detail line now correctly reads '...last verify: failed', BUT the badge is still 'Saved in app' (green) and the action button is still 'Connected · Manage' in the same green success styling used for a genuinely working provider -- confirmed via GET /agents/providers: {"id":"anthropic","status":"connected","lastVerifyStatus":"failed",...}. Root cause: apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:1713-1723 (_build_provider_entry) sets status='connected' unconditionally whenever a DB credential row exists (`if db: source='database'; status='connected'`), and the only downgrade path checks a separate, unrelated persisted admin `override` row (line 1746), never `last_verify_status`. The frontend (apps/web/src/components/agents/ProviderConnections.tsx:15-31, DOT/CARD_BORDER/ACTION_CLS) keys all styling strictly off `status`, so it has no way to reflect a failed verify short of the small unstyled detail sentence.

6. Reproduced in a SECOND, independent fresh browser session: the mismatch was observed persisted (no click) at load, then a second live Test-connection call was fired and returned the identical HTTP 401 failure, and the badge/action again remained green 'Connected · Manage' afterward.
7. Confirmed via a fresh page reload / browser back-navigation to the same route: the misleading green state survives a full reload (it is server-persisted, not a stale client cache).
findings.expected: A provider whose most recent live verify failed should visually demote to the existing 'warning' state (yellow dot/badge, e.g. matching the wireframe's 'Re-authenticate' treatment already used for Google Gemini) rather than remain in the 'connected'/green state, so a paying user is not misled into thinking Anthropic-routed agent runs will succeed.
findings.observed: status stays 'connected' (green, 'Connected · Manage') indefinitely regardless of lastVerifyStatus; only a small, neutral-colored detail sentence carries the true state, and the one-time toast notice produced by clicking Test Connection is transient and disappears on the next page load, leaving only the misleading green badge as the persistent, at-a-glance signal.

findings.summary: The desktop topbar's account-identity chip (avatar 'AD' + 'Administrator' name + 'Administrator' role, two lines) renders unconditionally at 390px width — it has no responsive hide class, unlike its sibling search box in the same header which correctly carries 'max-lg:hidden'. This crowds the header's left-side greeting text into a narrower column and is a direct, newly-identified contributing cause of the previously-filed header clipping bug (MV-mobile-dashboard-001), which this paid re-test confirms still reproduces byte-for-byte with real content present.

2. Navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard.

Inspect the <header> element's DOM: it contains three children — (a) greeting/subtitle text block, (b) a search <input> with class 'w-72 max-lg:hidden' (correctly hidden at mobile widths, confirmed 0-width in computed layout), and (c) a user-identity chip '<div class="flex items-center gap-2.5 pl-3 border-l border-white/10">' containing avatar 'AD' + 'Administrator' (name) + 'Administrator' (role) — this chip carries NO responsive hide class and is fully visible/rendered at 390px (confirmed via getBoundingClientRect: top:16.5 bottom:46.5, within the header).
