Cross-check MV-mobile-dashboard-001: measure h1 ('Good afternoon, Administrator') and p ('Fri, 17 July · last agent run Nm ago') rects — h1.top=-15 (15px clipped above the 64px-tall header box), p.bottom=78 (14px overflowing below the header) — identical to the original free-tier measurement, now confirmed with full paid content rendering behind it, and reproduced in a second fresh session (headerClipReproduced in session2-verify-summary.json: h1Top:-15, pBottom:78, both flags true).
findings.expected: Per design/screens/mobile-dashboard.html (data-design-id=m-topbar-md01), the mobile header contains only an avatar + single-line greeting on the left and the notification bell on the right — no duplicate account-identity block. At minimum, if the desktop topbar component is reused, its account-chip element should carry the same 'max-lg:hidden' (or equivalent) responsive treatment already correctly applied to its sibling search box.
findings.observed: The account-identity chip is unconditionally rendered at all viewport widths, redundantly repeating 'Administrator' a third time on screen (greeting says 'Good afternoon, Administrator'; chip says 'Administrator' / 'Administrator' again) while consuming header width that the greeting/subtitle text needs, worsening the pre-existing clip/overflow bug. Verified in two independent fresh sessions.

findings.summary: The dashboard shows three mutually-inconsistent counts for the same underlying concept ('how many jobs has this user applied to') on the same page, same session, same moment: the 'ACTIVE APPLICATIONS' stat card and 'Application Funnel > Applied' row both show 7 (sourced from GET /api/analytics/funnel: {"applied":7}), while 'Real-Time Market Pulse > Market vs. Your Performance > Applications / month' shows 'you 14' (sourced from GET /api/analytics/market-pulse: marketVsYou.comparisons[0].you=14). A direct query of the raw record store, GET /api/applications, returns a THIRD number: 12 records (6 submitted, 5 draft, 1 rejected). None of the three numbers agree.
reproduction: 1. Sign in as admin/admin123 (Pro entitlement) at 390x844, land on /dashboard.
2. Read 'ACTIVE APPLICATIONS' stat card: 7. Scroll to 'Application Funnel': 'Applied' row also reads 7.
3. Continue scrolling to 'Real-Time Market Pulse > Market vs. Your Performance': 'Applications / month ... you 14'.
4. With the same session token, call GET /api/analytics/funnel directly -> {"jobs_found":44,"applied":7,...} (matches stat card/funnel).
5. Call GET /api/analytics/market-pulse directly -> marketVsYou.comparisons contains {"label":"Applications / month","you":14} (matches the Market Pulse widget, contradicting the funnel's 7).
6. Call GET /api/applications directly (with limit=1000 to rule out pagination) -> returns exactly 12 application records, matching neither 7 nor 14.

7. Repeated in a fresh session 2 (session2-verify-summary.json): activeApplicationsCard=7, funnelApplied=7, and body-text re-confirms 'you 14' under Market vs. Your Performance — identical inconsistency reproduced.
findings.expected: A single, internally-consistent 'applications' figure — or at minimum, distinctly-labelled metrics with an explanation of why they differ (e.g., '7 this cycle vs 14 all-time') should not silently disagree without a period/definition label distinguishing them, since both widgets appear on the identical unfiltered dashboard view for the same time period.
findings.observed: GET /api/analytics/funnel and GET /api/analytics/market-pulse compute 'applications' using different logic/source and disagree (7 vs 14); a third source of truth (the raw /api/applications table, 12 records) agrees with neither. This is real, dynamically-fetched data (not hardcoded — confirmed against wireframe placeholder values which are completely different, and against fixture-string greps which found no matches), so the 'is it real data' check passes, but the paying user is shown self-contradictory numbers about their own core usage metric on the primary hub screen with no explanation.

findings.summary: Now that Pro entitlement makes the dashboard hub reachable, this confirms production does NOT implement the wireframe's purpose-built, glanceable mobile hub (design/screens/mobile-dashboard.html: compact 2x2 stat grid + 1 approval banner + 3-item activity feed, fitting in roughly one screen height). Instead /dashboard serves the entire desktop dashboard (11 widgets: stats, filterable activity feed, Today's Opportunities, Application Funnel, Story Bank, Recruiter CRM, Needs Approval, and a 6-part Real-Time Market Pulse block) reflowed to a single 390px-wide column, 6382px tall (~7.5x the 844px viewport).
reproduction: 1. Sign in as admin/admin123 (Pro entitlement) at 390x844, land on /dashboard.
2. Query all elements with a data-design-id attribute (the wireframe's own component markers): only m-notif-md02 (bell) and m-tabbar-md08 (bottom nav) are present in the DOM.

3. m-topbar-md01 (compact header), m-main-md03 (main content wrapper), m-stats-md04 (2x2 stat grid), m-approval-md05/btn-review-md06 (single approval banner), and m-feed-md07 (3-item activity feed) are entirely absent — the actual header and main content use different, desktop-shared component markup instead.
4. Measure document.documentElement.scrollHeight = 6382px vs. viewport 844px — the user must scroll roughly 7.5 full screens to reach the bottom of the hub.

5. Confirm the additional desktop widgets not in the wireframe are all present and functioning at mobile width: Today's Opportunities, Application Funnel, Story Bank, Recruiter CRM, Needs Approval, Real-Time Market Pulse (Trend Indicators, Jobs by Source donut, Top Skills, Job Probability Score, Weekly Activity heatmap, Employer Hiring Activity, Recruiter Activity chart, Market vs. Your Performance).
findings.expected: A dedicated, compact mobile hub as specified in the wireframe — quick stats, one approval prompt, a short activity feed — optimized for a quick mobile glance, with deeper content reachable via the other tab-bar destinations (Jobs/Apps/Agents/Profile) rather than all on one infinite-scroll home screen.
findings.observed: The full desktop dashboard's component tree is reused verbatim and reflowed responsively for mobile. This is NOT broken — verified zero horizontal overflow (overflow-check.json: overflowCount=0), clean single-column card stacking at every scroll depth (p06-scroll-01 through 08), no touch targets under 44x44 (touch-targets.json: 0 of 38 too small) — but it is a significant, systematic deviation from the wireframe's design intent for a 'quick mobile glance' pattern, and directly explains why MV-mobile-dashboard-003 (the free-tier coverage-gap finding) could not have anticipated this: the hidden content is ~8x larger and structurally different from what the wireframe specifies.
findings.status: CONDITIONALLY-CLOSED-BLOCKED-ON-HUMAN (H-4 freemium: user-facing dishonesty cured by cluster-G honest pricing copy + cluster-D settings/billing always-reachable; whether free-tier gets the full dashboard/5-runs is the owner's D1 decision per ADR-MV-02).

findings.summary: funnel_sankey() (apps/api/app/routers/applications.py) computes each funnel stage as an exact current-status bucket, so under realistic aging-pipeline data (apps that advanced past intermediate stages, leaving those buckets empty while later stages are populated) node-to-node dropoffs (stage_N - stage_{N+1}) go NEGATIVE — e.g. 3 apps at 'interview' + 1 at 'offer' with 0 at 'screening' yields a screened->interviewed dropoff of -3, which SankeyFlow.tsx:104 (`-{f.dropoff.count}`) renders as the broken literal string '−-3 · no response / screened out'. LIVE-reproduced on main c5ae389 (currently shipping). Same root cause as MV-application-tracker-005; single fix = switch funnel_sankey to the cumulative model (each node = apps that reached at least that stage, mirroring funnel()'s nested-IN) so dropoffs are monotonic non-negative, plus a render clamp guard.
reproduction: Seed 3 Applications at status='interview' + 1 at 'offer' (0 at 'screening') for a user
GET /api/applications/funnel/sankey with that user's token
Observe screened->interviewed dropoff = -3; UI SankeyFlow renders '−-3 · <reason>'
findings.expected: Funnel dropoffs are always >= 0 (monotonic funnel); no '−-N' string ever renders.
findings.observed: Dropoff -3 returned; broken '−-3' render.

findings.summary: JD Keyword Coverage still surfaces CamelCase CONCATENATION artifacts as top chips (e.g. 'ManagerLocation' as #1 for the Empire-Life pattern; 'EngineerLocation','ResponsibilitiesBuild','EmployerShare' for other scraped JDs). These are HTML-scrape gluings (a heading word concatenated to the next). Root cause: _skill_score rewards internal-uppercase (+45), so concatenation artifacts rank high. Fix: detect/split CamelCase concatenations where the split yields a known non-skill/boilerplate word (Location/Share/Build/Responsibilities/Employer) and drop or split them; do not reward internal-uppercase for such gluings. NOT a PII leak. Consolidates the re-opened MV-cover-letter-studio-006.
reproduction: Paste a scraped JD (Empire-Life pattern) into cover-letter studio
Observe 'ManagerLocation' as the #1 keyword chip
findings.expected: Keyword chips are plausible skills/terms; no CamelCase concatenation artifacts.
findings.observed: 'ManagerLocation' etc. surface as top chips.
findings.owner: fix — split/filter CamelCase JD-keyword concatenation artifacts

findings.summary: scripts/discovery_cron.sh swallows its own failure diagnostics: http_call()'s FATAL branch calls log(), which echoes to the function's stdout — but every caller captures that stdout via command substitution (LOGIN_RESP=$(http_call ...)), so FATAL lines never reach /var/log/aether/discovery.log. Result: 48+ hours of failed firings produced ZERO new log bytes, masking a full discovery outage (MV-system-006).
reproduction: 1. bash -x scripts/discovery_cron.sh with an invalid credential → http_call hits FATAL branch, log() output captured into LOGIN_RESP
2. tail /var/log/aether/discovery.log → no new line for the failed run (confirmed live: manual firing 2026-07-20T01:06:01Z exit 1, zero log bytes)
findings.expected: Every cron failure writes a timestamped FATAL line to discovery.log (diagnostics must survive command substitution — write to stderr and/or append directly to the log file)
findings.observed: Failures are silent; outage since 2026-07-18T00:31:18Z was invisible in the log
findings.verifiedBy: reviewer PASS (review-mv-system-008-b08b502.json) + deployer live firing 03:08:51Z: timestamped FATAL line landed in /var/log/aether/discovery.log (previously silent class); merged @e182571
